topic: B2B Demand Generation and Buyer Enablement
headline: Google Says AI Visibility Hinges On Content People Actually Want To Read via @sejournal, @martinibuster
keyNotes: Surprisingly, Google says that businesses are losing website visitors not just because of new AI search tools, but also because people are choosing to watch videos and use social media more often instead of reading traditional text.
To be seen by new AI search tools, your content needs to be truly unique and show real expertise, not just a "1,000th copy" of something already widely available.
It's crucial to make sure Google can easily find and read your content; if you block it from being accessed, it won't show up in search results.

Instead of trying to create content specifically to please search engines, focus on making content that your actual audience genuinely loves and finds useful, as this is what will ultimately get people to click and read.
question: The article emphasizes that content should be unique and offer "interesting expertise" that people actually want to read, rather than being a "1,000th copy." How might your business identify and share its truly unique insights or experiences that no one else can offer, to stand out in a crowded online world?
